title: Stackydo Config
description: User-facing configuration for a stackydo project or workspace. Place as `stackydo.json` in your project root, or in your home directory as `~/.stackydo.json` for a global fallback. stackydo discovers the file by walking up from the current working directory and falling back to home. This is the only configuration file users need to edit; the workspace's `manifest.json` is internal state and managed automatically.

$schema.description: JSON Schema URI (for editor autocompletion and validation).

dir.description: Path to the task store directory, relative to this file. Overrides the STACKYDO_DIR environment variable. The directory will be created on first task save if it does not exist.

stack_filter.description: Glob pattern (supports `*` wildcards) limiting which stacks appear in list, stats, stacks, and search output. When absent, all stacks are shown. Does not restrict write access — for true isolation, use separate workspaces.

context.description: Project context metadata captured into each task's `context` field at creation time. Useful for giving AI agents background about the project a task belongs to.

description.description: Free-text notes captured as context when creating tasks. Useful for giving AI agents background about this project.

workflow.description:
Workspace default workflow. Replaces the built-in `kanban` workflow as the default for any stack that is not assigned a workflow in `stack_workflows`. Use this to define custom statuses or aliases that fit your team's process.

When omitted, the built-in `kanban` workflow is used.

stack_workflows.description:
Per-stack workflow assignment. Keys are stack names; values are the names of built-in workflows. Stacks not listed here use the workspace default (`workflow` if set, otherwise `kanban`).

Matching is case-insensitive. Currently supported workflow names: `kanban`, `deck`.

additionalProperties.description: Name of a built-in workflow to apply to this stack.

stage.description: Fixed lifecycle stage. Every task status maps to exactly one stage. The stage drives system behavior: `archive` tasks are hidden from list/search by default, `active` tasks are eligible for overdue calculation, etc.

workflowConfig.description: Maps user-defined status strings to fixed lifecycle stages. Use this to define a custom status set for your workspace (or for a single stack via `stack_workflows`).

statuses.description: Map of status name → stage. The status name is what gets stored on each task (e.g. `"todo"`, `"in_progress"`). Each status must map to one of the three fixed stages: `backlog`, `active`, or `archive`. Define at least one status per stage you intend to use; at minimum, define one `backlog` status that new tasks can default to.

aliases.description: Optional input aliases. Maps alias → canonical status name. Aliases are resolved on input (e.g. `stackydo update SD1 --status doing`) but tasks are stored under the canonical name. Useful for accepting common typos or shorthand.

context.description: Customer-facing web app. Tasks here usually relate to bug fixes or feature work in the main repo.
